overview: This is a female Scorpion-tailed Spider (Arachnura genus) from the orb-weaver family. The bright yellow, tail-like structure is an elongated abdomen used to mimic debris, flowers, or leaves to camouflage against predators. Despite their intimidating appearance, they are relatively harmless to humans and are not venomous stingers. They are found across Australasia, Southern and Eastern Asia, with one species located in Africa and Madagascar. Wikipedia +5 5 sites Arachnura - Wikipedia

This is a female Scorpion-tailed Spider (Arachnura genus) from the orb-weaver family.
The bright yellow, tail-like structure is an elongated abdomen used to mimic debris, flowers, or leaves to camouflage against predators.
Despite their intimidating appearance, they are relatively harmless to humans and are not venomous stingers.
They are found across Australasia, Southern and Eastern Asia, with one species located in Africa and Madagascar.
